---
title: "Troubleshooting Shiny App: Update Network Plots"
format: html
---

AI generated version of function: 

# Set up

```{r}
# language: r
# plot_comparable_networks_tidy.R
library(tidyverse)
library(igraph)
library(cowplot) # for arranging grid output
library(viridis) # for color scales
library(scales)
library(here)
library(tidygraph)

source(here::here('R/color_network_nodes.R'))
```

```{r}
indir_ports = 'data/networks/participation_vessel_ports'


processdir <- "data/input"
```
```{r}

gdp_defl <- read.csv(
  file = paste(here(processdir), "inflation_adjustment.csv", sep = "/")
)
```

```{r}
years <- seq(2004, 2025)
# years <- 2008 # testing script, 1/6
# years <- 2018 # testing script, 1/8
# years <- 2014 # testing script, 1/27

## port groups
myports <- c(
  "Puget Sound",
  "North WA Coast",
  "WA Coast",
  "Other Coastal Washington",
  "Astoria",
  "Tillamook",
  "Columbia River",
  "Newport",
  "Coos Bay",
  "Brookings",
  "Crescent City",
  "Eureka",
  "Morro Bay",
  "Fort Bragg",
  "Bodega Bay",
  "San Francisco",
  "San Diego",
  "Monterey",
  "Santa Barbara",
  "Los Angeles",
  "Unknown Ports"
)
```
```{r}
total_rev_cutoff_2005 <- 5000 #USD in 2005; following Holland et al. diversification indices
indiv_rev_cutoff_2005 <- 500 #USD in 2005; following Holland et al. diversification indices

gdp_defl_2005 <- gdp_defl %>% filter(year == 2005) %>% pull() # generated in script 00_process

total_rev_cutoff <- total_rev_cutoff_2005 / gdp_defl_2005
indiv_rev_cutoff <- indiv_rev_cutoff_2005 / gdp_defl_2005


edgetype <- "connectivity"
## To avoid inclusion of rare or minimal fishing activity, a given fishery must contribute to at least contr_cutoff% of a port's seasonal revenue to be included in the network data. This cutoff value can be increased if more stringent fishery inclusion criteria are preferred, and decreased (i.e. for summaries over shorter temporal / smaller spatial scales).
contr_cutoff <- 0.1
```
```{r}
most_recent_igraph <- '2026-03-10' # set date associated with igraph files

graphs_list <- list()
graph_names <- c()
i=1
for(iopac in myports){
  for(y in years){
  #y=2013 # testing script for single node networks, 1/7
  # y=2014 # testing script for single node networks, 1/27
    if(file.exists(here::here(indir_ports,paste0("igraph_", iopac, "_", y, "_totalrev", total_rev_cutoff,"_indivrev", indiv_rev_cutoff,"_", edgetype,"_rmCrab_",most_recent_igraph,".rds")))){
      tmpgraph <- readRDS(here::here(indir_ports,paste0("igraph_", iopac, "_", y, "_totalrev", total_rev_cutoff,"_indivrev", indiv_rev_cutoff,"_", edgetype,"_rmCrab_",most_recent_igraph,".rds")))
      V(tmpgraph)$p <- iopac
      V(tmpgraph)$year <- y
      V(tmpgraph)$colors <- vertex_color(tmpgraph)
      graphs_list[[i]] <- tmpgraph
      graph_names[i] <- paste0(iopac,"_",y)
      i = i + 1
      } else{ y = y +1 }
  }
}
names(graphs_list) <- graph_names

```
## Simple plotting function

```{r}
To create a simplified version, we can strip away the complex file-saving logic and the manual matrix aggregation. Instead, we can use ggraph, which is the standard tidyverse-compatible library for network visualization in R.

The following function takes a list of networks and plots them using a fixed layout, ensuring that Node A is always in the same spot across all plots.

The Simplified R Function
R
library(tidyverse)
library(tidygraph)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)

plot_networks_simple <- function(glist, layout_type = "stress") {
  # 1. Create a "Master Graph" to define the fixed layout
  # This ensures nodes don't jump around between different plots
  master_graph <- do.call(igraph::union, glist)
  
  # 2. Pre-calculate coordinates
  fixed_layout <- create_layout(master_graph, layout = layout_type)
  
  # 3. Define a plotting function for a single graph
  draw_graph <- function(g, name) {
    # Copy the fixed coordinates to the specific graph
    # (Node names must match for this to align correctly)
    curr_layout <- fixed_layout %>%
      filter(name %in% V(g)$name)
    
    ggraph(g, layout = "manual", x = curr_layout$x, y = curr_layout$y) +
      geom_edge_link(aes(edge_alpha = weight), color = "grey70", show.legend = FALSE) +
      geom_node_point(aes(size = importance), color = "steelblue") +
      geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
      theme_graph() +
      labs(title = paste("Network:", name))
  }
  
  # 4. Map the function over your list and return the plots
  plot_list <- imap(glist, ~draw_graph(.x, .y))
  return(plot_list)
}
```
## Tidy Plotting Function

```{r}
plot_comparable_networks_tidy <- function(
  glist,
  color_labels = FALSE,
  outdir = NULL,
  file_suffix = paste0("_compare_tidy"),
  individual = TRUE,
  grid = FALSE,
  grid_layout = c(2, 2),
  layout = c("circle", "fr"),
  width = 10,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
) {
  layout <- match.arg(layout)
  if (!is.list(glist) || length(glist) == 0) {
    abort("glist must be a non-empty list of igraph objects")
  }
  # 1) unified node set
  all_nodes <- unique(unlist(lapply(glist, function(g) V(g)$name)))
  n_nodes <- length(all_nodes)
  # 2) aggregated adjacency (sum of weighted adjacency matrices)
  agg_mat <- matrix(
    0,
    nrow = n_nodes,
    ncol = n_nodes,
    dimnames = list(all_nodes, all_nodes)
  )
  for (g in glist) {
    if (ecount(g) == 0) {
      next
    }
    # adjacency with node ordering matched to all_nodes
    w <- igraph::as_adjacency_matrix(g, attr = "weight", sparse = FALSE)
    # w may not have all rows/cols; match by names
    if (is.null(rownames(w))) {
      next
    }
    common <- intersect(rownames(w), all_nodes)
    agg_mat[common, common] <- agg_mat[common, common] + w[common, common]
  }
  agg_graph <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(
    agg_mat,
    mode = "undirected",
    weighted = TRUE,
    diag = FALSE
  )
  # 3) compute stable layout (x,y) once
  if (layout == "circle") {
    # order nodes by name for reproducibility
    order_idx <- seq_along(all_nodes)
    circ_coords <- layout_in_circle(agg_graph, order = order_idx)
    layout_df <- tibble(
      name = V(agg_graph)$name,
      x = circ_coords[, 1],
      y = circ_coords[, 2]
    )
  } else {
    # force reproducible layout by setting seed
    set.seed(42)
    fr_coords <- layout_with_fr(agg_graph)
    layout_df <- tibble(
      name = V(agg_graph)$name,
      x = fr_coords[, 1],
      y = fr_coords[, 2]
    )
  }
  # scale layout to unit circle range for nicer offsets
  layout_df <- layout_df %>%
    mutate(x = rescale(x, to = c(-1, 1)), y = rescale(y, to = c(-1, 1)))
  # compute angles for radial label placement
  layout_df <- layout_df %>%
    mutate(angle = atan2(y, x), angle_deg = angle * 180 / pi)
  # helper: safe vertex attribute extraction, returns tibble with node names
  vertex_attrs_from_graph <- function(g) {
    vnm <- V(g)$name
    tibble(
      name = vnm,
      # many graphs have attributes like common_name, vessels, importance, percent_size, colors, year, p
      common_name = if ("common_name" %in% vertex_attr_names(g)) {
        vertex_attr(g, "common_name")
      } else {
        vnm
      },
      vessels = if ("vessels" %in% vertex_attr_names(g)) {
        as.numeric(vertex_attr(g, "vessels"))
      } else {
        0
      },
      importance = if ("importance" %in% vertex_attr_names(g)) {
        as.numeric(vertex_attr(g, "importance"))
      } else {
        0
      },
      percent_size = if ("percent_size" %in% vertex_attr_names(g)) {
        as.numeric(vertex_attr(g, "percent_size"))
      } else {
        0
      },
      colors = if ("colors" %in% vertex_attr_names(g)) {
        vertex_attr(g, "colors")
      } else {
        NA_character_
      },
      year = if ("year" %in% vertex_attr_names(g)) {
        as.character(vertex_attr(g, "year"))
      } else {
        NA_character_
      },
      p = if ("p" %in% vertex_attr_names(g)) {
        as.character(vertex_attr(g, "p"))
      } else {
        NA_character_
      }
    )
  }
  # function to build ggplot for a single igraph object
  plot_one <- function(g) {
    # vertex data
    vdf <- tibble(name = all_nodes) %>%
      left_join(vertex_attrs_from_graph(g), by = "name") %>%
      left_join(layout_df, by = "name") %>%
      replace_na(list(
        common_name = NA_character_,
        vessels = 0,
        importance = 0,
        percent_size = 0,
        colors = NA_character_,
        year = NA_character_,
        p = NA_character_
      ))
    # ensure numeric size: use importance if >0 else percent_size scaled else vessels
    vdf <- vdf %>%
      mutate(
        size_proxy = case_when(
          importance > 0 ~ importance,
          percent_size > 0 ~ percent_size *
            max(1, max(percent_size, na.rm = TRUE)),
          TRUE ~ pmax(1, vessels)
        )
      )
    # assign colors: preserve provided colors if available; otherwise use viridis mapped to size_proxy
    missing_color_idx <- is.na(vdf$colors) | vdf$colors == ""
    if (any(missing_color_idx)) {
      vdf$colors[missing_color_idx] <- viridis(n_nodes)[which(
        missing_color_idx
      )]
    }
    # edge data for this graph
    if (ecount(g) > 0) {
      edf_raw <- as_data_frame(g, what = "edges") %>% as_tibble()
      # endpoints may be numeric indices or names depending on as_data_frame; coerce
      if (!"from_name" %in% names(edf_raw)) {
        edf_raw <- edf_raw %>%
          mutate(from = as.character(from), to = as.character(to))
      }
      edf <- edf_raw %>%
        select(from = from, to = to, weight = weight) %>%
        mutate(from = as.character(from), to = as.character(to)) %>%
        # keep only edges between nodes in all_nodes
        filter(from %in% all_nodes, to %in% all_nodes) %>%
        left_join(
          layout_df %>% rename(x = x, y = y),
          by = c("from" = "name")
        ) %>%
        rename(x = x, y = y) %>%
        left_join(
          layout_df %>% rename(xend = x, yend = y),
          by = c("to" = "name")
        )
    } else {
      edf <- tibble(
        from = character(),
        to = character(),
        weight = numeric(),
        x = numeric(),
        y = numeric(),
        xend = numeric(),
        yend = numeric()
      )
    }
    # compute label positions offset from center along radial direction
    max_range <- max(abs(layout_df$x), abs(layout_df$y))
    label_offset <- 0.18 * max_range
    vdf <- vdf %>%
      mutate(
        label_x = x + cos(angle) * (label_offset + 0.02 * size_proxy),
        label_y = y + sin(angle) * (label_offset + 0.02 * size_proxy),
        cent_label = if_else(vessels > 0, paste0("(", vessels, ")"), ""),
        # decide hjust/vjust based on angle so labels don't overlap nodes
        hjust = case_when(
          abs(angle_deg) <= 90 ~ 0,
          TRUE ~ 1
        )
      )
    # build ggplot
    p <- ggplot() +
      # edges: weight -> alpha/size; small minimum for visibility
      geom_segment(
        data = edf,
        aes(
          x = x,
          y = y,
          xend = xend,
          yend = yend,
          size = pmax(weight, .0001),
          alpha = pmin(weight / max(weight, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
        ),
        colour = "grey60",
        lineend = "round"
      ) +
      scale_size_continuous(range = c(0.25, 2.5), guide = "none") +
      scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.2, 0.9), guide = "none") +
      # nodes
      geom_point(
        data = vdf,
        aes(x = x, y = y, size = size_proxy, fill = colors),
        shape = 21,
        colour = "black",
        stroke = 0.3
      ) +
      scale_fill_identity() +
      scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 18), guide = "none") +
      # center small vessel label
      geom_text(
        data = vdf %>% filter(cent_label != ""),
        aes(x = x, y = y, label = cent_label),
        size = 3,
        colour = "black",
        vjust = 0.5
      ) +
      # outer fishery name labels
      geom_text(
        data = vdf,
        aes(
          x = label_x,
          y = label_y,
          label = common_name %||% name,
          colour = if (color_labels) colors else "gray25"
        ),
        size = 3.25,
        hjust = vdf$hjust,
        family = "sans"
      ) +
      scale_colour_identity() +
      coord_fixed(expand = TRUE) +
      theme_void() +
      labs(
        title = paste0(
          ifelse(is.na(unique(vdf$p)[1]), "", paste0(unique(vdf$p)[1], " ")),
          ifelse(is.na(unique(vdf$year)[1]), "", paste0(unique(vdf$year)[1])),
          "  "
        ),
        caption = NULL
      )
    return(p)
  }
  # produce plots for each graph
  plots <- map(glist, plot_one)
  names(plots) <- map_chr(
    glist,
    ~ {
      v <- vertex_attr(.x)
      yr <- if ("year" %in% names(v)) unique(v$year)[1] else NA_character_
      p <- if ("p" %in% names(v)) unique(v$p)[1] else NA_character_
      paste0(coalesce(p, ""), if (!is.na(yr)) paste0("_", yr) else "")
    }
  )
  # save individual plots if requested
  if (individual && !is.null(outdir)) {
    dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
    walk2(plots, names(plots), function(pl, nm) {
      fname <- make.names(paste0(nm, "_", file_suffix, ".png"))
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(outdir, fname),
        plot = pl,
        width = width,
        height = height,
        dpi = dpi,
        units = "in",
        bg = "transparent"
      )
    })
  }
  # save grid if requested
  if (grid && !is.null(outdir)) {
    dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
    # arrange plots, fill with empty plots if not enough
    n_needed <- prod(grid_layout)
    pls <- plots
    if (length(pls) < n_needed) {
      empty <- ggplot() + theme_void()
      pls <- c(pls, rep(list(empty), n_needed - length(pls)))
    }
    grid_plot <- plot_grid(
      plotlist = pls[1:n_needed],
      nrow = grid_layout[1],
      ncol = grid_layout[2]
    )
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(
        outdir,
        paste0("MultiNetwork_", file_suffix, ".png")
      ),
      plot = grid_plot,
      width = width,
      height = height,
      dpi = dpi,
      units = "in",
      bg = "transparent"
    )
  }
  invisible(plots)
}
```

```{r}
pngdir_ports_tidy = 'data/networks/participation_vessel_ports/plots/comparable_tidy'

plot_comparable_networks_tidy(
  glist = graphs_list[1:20],
  outdir = pngdir_ports_tidy
)
```

```{r}

pngdir_ports = 'data/networks/participation_vessel_ports/plots/comparable'


plot_comparable_networks(
  graphs_list,
  outdir = pngdir_ports,
  color_labels = FALSE
)
```

## Old code for function

Plot Comparable Participation Networks
```{r} 
#'
#' Use igraph objects to plot a series of fisheries participation
#' networks that have consistent node placement across plots.
#'
#' Version 2 uses a re-scaling function to plot the node labels at a
#'        consistent distance / direction out from the center of each node,
#'        to make the graphs easier to read. Main labels consist only of the
#'        fishery name; vessel counts associated with the fishery are placed
#'        at the center of the fishery node, in a smaller text.
#'
#' @param g network as an igraph object
#' @param color_labels whether the label colors should match the node colors, or be a consistent dark gray text (TRUE/FALSE). If TRUE, label colors are assigned with V(g)$colors
#' @param outdir directory where image files will be saved
#' @param file_suffix string to be added to the suffix of the image file name. script will automatically name the files by 'port_group' and year. 'port_group' can be defined as desired based on unique(V(g)$p)
#' @param individual save each network plot to its own png file (TRUE/FALSE)
#' @param grid plot networks in a single png file, using a grid (facets). Not recommended for more than four networks. (TRUE/FALSE)
#' @param grid_layout layout for the multi-plot png, if grid=TRUE. Should be in format: c(nrows,ncolumns)
#' @return Null. Will write plots directly to files
#' @examples
#' plot_comparable_networks(graphs_list, outdir="data/networks/participation/plots/comparable")
#' @export
plot_comparable_networks <- function(
  glist,
  color_labels = FALSE,
  outdir,
  file_suffix = paste0("_compare"),
  individual = TRUE,
  grid = FALSE,
  grid_layout = c(2, 2)
) {
  ######## set up - functions ########
  # get the vertex IDs for a graph
  get_vid <- function(igraph_obj) {
    vertex_attr(igraph_obj)[[1]]
  }

  # add () to vessel counts, and remove 0 vessel counts, for plotting
  add_vessel_labels <- function(igraph_obj) {
    label_vec <- c()
    for (i in seq(1, length(V(igraph_obj)$name))) {
      if (V(igraph_obj)$vessels[i] != 0) {
        label_vec[i] <- paste0(" (", V(igraph_obj)$vessels[i], ")")
      } else {
        label_vec[i] <- ""
      }
    }
    return(label_vec)
  }

  # re-scaling function to set positions of node labels. from: https://gist.github.com/kjhealy/834774
  radian.rescale <- function(x, start = 0, direction = 1) {
    c.rotate <- function(x) (x + start) %% (2 * pi) * direction
    c.rotate(scales::rescale(x, c(0, 2 * pi), range(x)))
  }

  ###################################

  # get all the unique node IDs from the list of graphs you want to compare
  unique_node_ids <- unique(unlist(lapply(glist, get_vid)))

  # build a matrix to see which graphs have which nodes
  node_mat <- matrix(
    nrow = length(unique_node_ids),
    ncol = length(glist),
    dimnames = list(c(unique_node_ids), c(seq(1, length(glist))))
  )
  for (i in seq(1, dim(node_mat)[2])) {
    # for each graph
    for (j in seq(1, dim(node_mat)[1])) {
      # for each node
      tmp_g <- glist[[i]]
      tmp_node <- unique_node_ids[j]
      if (tmp_node %in% get_vid(tmp_g)) {
        node_mat[j, i] <- 1
      } else {
        node_mat[j, i] <- 0
      }
    }
  }

  # if any graphs are missing a node, add it in as a blank
  new_glist = list()
  for (i in seq(1, dim(node_mat)[2])) {
    tmp_g <- glist[[i]]
    if (any(node_mat[, i] == 0)) {
      # identify the missing nodes
      missing_nodes <- c(unique_node_ids[which(
        !(unique_node_ids %in% get_vid(tmp_g))
      )])
      for (j in seq(1, length(missing_nodes))) {
        tmp_node <- missing_nodes[j]

        # get attributes from a graph that does have that node
        ref_g <- glist[[which(node_mat[tmp_node, ] == 1)[[1]]]]
        ref_attr <- vertex_attr(ref_g, index = which(V(ref_g)$name == tmp_node))

        # reset graph-specific attributes
        # -- to zero
        ref_attr$size <- 0
        ref_attr$percent_size <- 0
        ref_attr$importance <- 0
        ref_attr$vessels <- 0
        # -- to reflect new graph
        ref_attr$p <- unique(V(tmp_g)$p)
        ref_attr$year <- unique(V(tmp_g)$year)
        ref_attr$fleet <- unique(V(tmp_g)$fleet)

        # add new node to graph
        if (j == 1) {
          new_tmp_g <- add_vertices(tmp_g, nv = 1, attr = ref_attr)
        } else {
          new_tmp_g <- add_vertices(new_tmp_g, nv = 1, attr = ref_attr)
        }
      } # end for(j)
      new_glist[[i]] <- new_tmp_g
      message("added ", length(missing_nodes), " empty vertices to graph ", i)
    } else {
      new_glist[[i]] <- tmp_g
    } # end if(any == 0)
  } # for(i)

  # plot each graph, using the same order of nodes
  if (individual) {
    for (i in seq(1, length(new_glist))) {
      g <- new_glist[[i]]
      # get port group / year info from graph object
      port_group <- unique(V(g)$p)
      y <- unique(V(g)$year)
      # prepare the layout with the `layout_in_circle` function, which allows you to specify vertex order for plot
      new_order <- match(unique_node_ids, V(g)$name)
      l <- layout_in_circle(g, order = new_order)
      # remove labels for empty nodes
      V(g)$common_name[which(V(g)$vessels == 0)] <- ""
      # add vertex attribute describing number of vessels active in each fishery, after filters
      V(g)$vessel_label <- add_vessel_labels(g)

      # split echinoderms and Prawns/shrimp vertex into two lines, because it's too long for our graphing method
      V(g)$common_name <- ifelse(
        V(g)$common_name == "Echinoderms",
        "Echino-\nderms",
        V(g)$common_name
      )
      V(g)$common_name <- ifelse(
        V(g)$common_name == "Prawns/shrimp",
        "Prawns/\nshrimp",
        V(g)$common_name
      )

      # set label locations. see note at end of function
      lab.position.df <- data.frame(
        node = seq(1:vcount(g)),
        position = new_order
      ) %>%
        arrange(position)
      lab.locs <- radian.rescale(
        x = lab.position.df$node,
        direction = -1,
        start = 0
      )

      # set label colors
      if (color_labels) {
        V(g)$label_colors <- V(g)$colors
      } else {
        V(g)$label_colors <- "gray25"
      }

      # plot & save to file
      png(
        here::here(
          outdir,
          paste0(
            port_group,
            "_",
            y,
            "_circular",
            file_suffix,
            "_",
            Sys.Date(),
            ".png"
          )
        ),
        bg = "transparent"
      ) # if this resolution isn't good enough, add: width = 2000, height = 1500,res=300
      if (vcount(g) == 1 | ecount(g) == 0) {
        plot(
          g,
          vertex.size = V(g)$importance / (max(V(g)$importance) * 0.02),
          layout = l, #where to put the nodes on the plot
          # edge.width = sqrt(E(g)$weight)/(0.037*max(sqrt(E(g)$weight))),
          edge.curved = F,
          axes = F,
          edge.color = brewer.pal(n = 11, name = "Set3")[9],
          vertex.label = str_wrap(V(g)$common_name, width = 6), # JS updated 01-29-2021
          vertex.color = adjustcolor(V(g)$colors, alpha.f = 0.90),
          vertex.label.family = 'sans',
          vertex.label.color = V(g)$label_colors,
          vertex.label.cex = 2, # changed from 1.4, AG 01042025
          vertex.frame.color = NA,
          vertex.label.dist = 5,
          vertex.label.degree = lab.locs
        )
        text(x = l[, 1], y = l[, 2], labels = V(g)$vessel_label)
        dev.off()
      } else {
        plot(
          g,
          vertex.size = V(g)$importance / (max(V(g)$importance) * 0.02),
          layout = l, #where to put the nodes on the plot
          edge.width = sqrt(E(g)$weight) / (0.037 * max(sqrt(E(g)$weight))),
          edge.curved = F,
          axes = F,
          edge.color = brewer.pal(n = 11, name = "Set3")[9],
          # vertex.label = remove_empty_labels(g),
          vertex.label = str_wrap(V(g)$common_name, width = 6), # JS updated 01-29-2021
          vertex.color = adjustcolor(V(g)$colors, alpha.f = 0.90),
          vertex.label.family = 'sans',
          vertex.label.color = V(g)$label_colors,
          vertex.label.cex = 2, # changed from 1.4, AG 01042025
          vertex.frame.color = NA,
          vertex.label.dist = 5,
          vertex.label.degree = lab.locs
        )
        text(x = l[, 1], y = l[, 2], labels = V(g)$vessel_label)
        dev.off()
      }
    } # end for(i in new_glist)
  }

  # plot all of the graphs in one png,
  if (grid) {
    # start png
    png(
      here::here(
        outdir,
        paste0("MultiNetwork_", file_suffix, "_", Sys.Date(), ".png")
      ),
      res = 200,
      height = 1800,
      width = 1800,
      bg = "transparent"
    ) # if this resolution isn't good enough, add: width = 2000, height = 1500,res=300
    # grid layout for png, with adjusted margins (mai)
    par(mfrow = grid_layout, mai = c(b = 0.25, l = 0, t = 0.25, r = 0))
    # plot each network
    for (i in seq(1, length(new_glist))) {
      g <- new_glist[[i]]
      # prepare the layout with the `layout_in_circle` function, which allows you to specify vertex order for plot
      new_order <- match(unique_node_ids, V(g)$name)
      l <- layout_in_circle(g, order = new_order)
      # remove labels for empty nodes
      V(g)$common_name[which(V(g)$vessels == 0)] <- ""
      # add vertex attribute describing number of vessels active in each fishery, after filters
      V(g)$vessel_label <- add_vessel_labels(g)
      # split echinoderms vertex into two lines, because it's too long for our graphing method
      V(g)$common_name <- ifelse(
        V(g)$common_name == "Echinoderms",
        "Echino-\nderms",
        V(g)$common_name
      )
      # set label locations. see note at end of function
      lab.position.df <- data.frame(
        node = seq(1:vcount(g)),
        position = new_order
      ) %>%
        arrange(position)
      lab.locs <- radian.rescale(
        x = lab.position.df$node,
        direction = -1,
        start = 0
      )
      # set label colors
      if (color_labels) {
        V(g)$label_colors <- V(g)$colors
      } else {
        V(g)$label_colors <- "gray25"
      }

      # create plot
      if (vcount(g) == 1 | ecount(g) == 0) {
        plot(
          g,
          vertex.size = V(g)$importance / (max(V(g)$importance) * 0.02),
          layout = l, #where to put the nodes on the plot
          # edge.width = sqrt(E(g)$weight)/(0.037*max(sqrt(E(g)$weight))),
          edge.curved = F,
          axes = F,
          edge.color = brewer.pal(n = 11, name = "Set3")[9],
          vertex.label = str_wrap(V(g)$common_name, width = 6), # JS updated 01-29-2021
          vertex.color = adjustcolor(V(g)$colors, alpha.f = 0.90),
          vertex.label.family = 'sans',
          vertex.label.color = V(g)$label_colors,
          vertex.label.cex = 2, # changed from 1.4, AG 01042025
          vertex.frame.color = NA,
          main = paste0(unique(V(g)$p), " ", unique(V(g)$year)), # main title
          margin = c(b = 0, l = -1, t = 0, r = -1), # adjust plot margins, to get rid of whitespace
          vertex.label.dist = 5,
          vertex.label.degree = lab.locs
        )
        text(x = l[, 1], y = l[, 2], labels = V(g)$vessel_label)
      } else {
        plot(
          g,
          vertex.size = V(g)$importance / (max(V(g)$importance) * 0.02),
          layout = l, #where to put the nodes on the plot
          edge.width = sqrt(E(g)$weight) / (0.037 * max(sqrt(E(g)$weight))),
          edge.curved = F,
          axes = F,
          edge.color = brewer.pal(n = 11, name = "Set3")[9],
          # vertex.label = remove_empty_labels(g),
          vertex.label = str_wrap(V(g)$common_name, width = 6), # JS updated 01-29-2021
          vertex.color = adjustcolor(V(g)$colors, alpha.f = 0.90),
          vertex.label.family = 'sans',
          vertex.label.color = V(g)$label_colors,
          vertex.label.cex = 2, # changed from 1.4, AG 01042025
          vertex.frame.color = NA,
          main = paste0(unique(V(g)$p), " ", unique(V(g)$year)), # main title
          margin = c(b = 0, l = -1, t = 0, r = -1), # adjust plot margins, to get rid of whitespace
          vertex.label.dist = 5,
          vertex.label.degree = lab.locs
        )
        text(x = l[, 1], y = l[, 2], labels = V(g)$vessel_label)
      }
    } # end for(i in new_glist)

    dev.off()
  } # end if(grid)
}

## a note on setting label locations: ##
# from the plot function arguments, igraph calculates the x/y position of the node labels using the following equations:
# x <- layout[, 1] + label.dist * cos(-label.degree)
# y <- layout[, 2] + label.dist * sin(-label.degree)
# plus some weird ratio to scale with node size. This means that label.degree, and the x/y values from the layout dataframe,
# are read in as vectors; and when we re-arrange the node order in the layout_in_circle function, each node's position
# in the layout dataframe stops matching the corresponding position in the label.degree vector.
# To fix this, the input vector for the rescale.radians function must be the node numbers in order of their position in the
# graph. Creating the lab.position.df dataframe, and arranging it by the position column (taken from new_order, used as an
# argument in the layout_in_circle fx), provides rescale.radians with the correct input vector.

```

Old code - application of function

```{r}

pngdir_ports = 'data/networks/participation_vessel_ports/plots/comparable'


plot_comparable_networks(
  graphs_list,
  outdir = pngdir_ports,
  color_labels = FALSE
)
```
